_meta.rule: Every entry cites a real source. Seed entries below are placeholder shapes — operator must replace placeholder citations with real ones before flipping narrator_visible=1 on shared/regional entries. private_family entries can use 'Interview with Kent/Janice Horne, recorded YYYY' once captured.

events.title: World War II begins (Europe)
events.summary: Germany invades Poland September 1939; Britain and France declare war. The US enters December 1941 after Pearl Harbor. For German-Russian families on the prairie this carried double weight — a war with Germany while their grandparents had fled the Russian empire, and many older neighbors still spoke German at home.
events.source_citation: World War II — standard reference (Encyclopedia Britannica, accessed 2026-05-05). Cohort context: Conzen, K. N. (1985), Germans from Russia in the United States.
events.notes: Seed; refine cohort context with a specific scholarly citation before shipping shared_regional version.

events.title: Northern Plains drought (1955–1959)
events.summary: A multi-year drought hit the northern Great Plains hard. North Dakota saw substantially reduced wheat and barley yields. Farm auctions accelerated; some families that had homesteaded in the early 1900s did not survive the period as operators.
events.source_citation: DRAFT — refine with USDA drought year records + ND State Climate Office data + a county-level source (e.g., McLean County agricultural extension reports).
events.notes: Operator-only until the source is upgraded to academic / historical_society + narrator_visible flipped to 1.

events.title: Garrison Dam completed
events.summary: Garrison Dam on the Missouri River was completed in 1953 and impoundment of Lake Sakakawea finished filling in the early 1960s. The project displaced communities along the river — including the Three Affiliated Tribes (Mandan, Hidatsa, Arikara) — and reshaped agricultural and recreational life for the region.
events.source_citation: DRAFT — replace with Bureau of Reclamation completion records + a State Historical Society of North Dakota citation. Verify exact completion year (1953 vs 1956 — appears in different sources differently depending on whether 'completion' means structure or full impoundment).

events.title: Sputnik launched
events.summary: October 1957 — the Soviet Union launched the first artificial satellite. The US response shaped Cold War prairie life through missile-silo construction across the northern plains, education-funding shifts, and increased rural civil-defense exercises.
events.source_citation: Sputnik launch (Encyclopedia Britannica, accessed 2026-05-05). Cohort consequence: NK Wilford, J. N. (1989), 'How Sputnik Changed America' — refine with academic source.
events.notes: Cohort-relevant because of the missile-silo build-out across ND/MT/SD that started in 1959–1962.

events.title: Germans from Russia Heritage Society founded
events.summary: The Germans from Russia Heritage Society was founded in Bismarck, North Dakota, in 1971. Its formation reflected a generational push to preserve language, foodways, recipes, and migration history before the elders who carried it as living memory passed.
events.source_citation: Germans from Russia Heritage Society of North Dakota — official founding date publication.
events.notes: Verify exact founding date with the society's own publications before flipping shared_regional.

events.title: 1973 oil crisis
events.summary: OPEC oil embargo October 1973. Gas prices rose sharply; rural communities dependent on automobile transit and farm machinery felt the cost squeeze especially hard. For families running grain operations the season's profitability shifted overnight.

events.title: 1980s farm crisis
events.summary: Sharply falling commodity prices, high interest rates, and over-leveraged land caused widespread farm foreclosures across the upper Midwest. Communities that had survived the 1955–1959 drought lost generations-old operations during this period. Bank closures, farm auctions, and rural-population decline accelerated.
events.source_citation: DRAFT — refine with USDA Economic Research Service publications + a North Dakota State University agricultural extension citation.
